concerns.text: Task T022 implements 'Spearman rank correlation' while Spec FR-004 and Constitution Principle VII explicitly mandate 'Pearson correlation'. This is a semantic weakening of the statistical requirement without a documented spec amendment.

Task T023 implements 'cluster-based permutation tests' for spatial FDR, whereas Spec FR-005 and Constitution Principle VII mandate the 'Benjamini-Hochberg' procedure. This violates the explicit statistical constraint in the spec.

Task T009 instructs downloading the 'GLOBAL scope (90°S-90°N)' dataset, directly contradicting Spec FR-009 (which requires execution within 6h/7GB RAM on CI) and the Plan's explicit restriction to a 'regional domain (20°N-60°N)'. The task silently relaxes the resource constraint by attempting an infeasible global download.

Task T019 adds 'linear detrending' to Z500 anomalies. Spec FR-003 defines anomalies strictly as 'subtracting the 1979–2023 monthly climatology'. The task introduces an unrequested transformation step, altering the data definition without spec authorization.

Task T026 expands validation to include 'regression against scalar indices' and 'spatial correlation with templates'. Spec FR-010 only requires 'cross-referencing spatial patterns with established teleconnection indices'. This adds implementation scope not authorized by the spec.

Task T034 uses 'CACHED preprocessed data' for sensitivity analysis. Spec FR-007 requires a full sensitivity sweep to verify robustness. Caching preprocessed data (which depends on the baseline threshold in T021) invalidates the sensitivity test for the detection step, effectively weakening the robustness requirement.
